perturbation.original_text: 1.5 Notwithstanding the aforementioned Confidential I n f o r m a t i o n s h a l l e x c l u d e information that: (i) is already in the public domain at the time of disclosure by the Discloser to the Recipient or thereafter enters the public domain without any breach of the terms of this Agreement; (ii) was already known by the Recipient before the m o m e n t o f d i s c l o s u r e (under evidence of reasonable proof or written record of such disclosure); (iii) is subsequently communicated to the Recipient without any obligation of confidence from a third party who is in lawful possession thereof and under no obligation of confidence to the Discloser; (iv) becomes publicly available by other means than a breach of the confidentiality obligations by the Recipient (not through fault or failure to act by the Recipient); (iv) is or has been developed independently by employees, consultants or agents of the Recipient (proved by reasonable means) without violation of the European IPR Helpdesk | Page 5 of 8 terms of this Agreement or reference or access to any Confidential Information pertaining to the Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 Notwithstanding the aforementioned Confidential Information shall exclude information that: (i) is already in the public domain. Additional conditions or considerations may be determined at the Discloser's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific exclusions to confidential information, the phrase 'Additional conditions or considerations may be determined at the Discloser's discretion.' now creates an in-text contradiction, as this suggests the Discloser can retroactively change the definition of excluded information, conflicting with the previously established framework for confidentiality. This affects enforcement because the scope of excluded information becomes uncertain, as other clauses still refer to the original exclusions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 The Recipient will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Recipient will inform them about the confidential quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence the Recipient will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non-use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 1.5 significantly alters the definition of 'Confidential Information' by removing specific exclusions and granting the Discloser discretion to add further conditions. This directly contradicts section 3.2, which relies on a previously defined, objective scope of confidential information. The change introduces uncertainty and the possibility of retroactive changes to what is considered confidential, making it impossible to ensure that the Recipient can reliably fulfill their confidentiality obligations as described in section 3.2. The explanation given is accurate: the changed text creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Purpose of the Disclosure of Confidential Information The Discloser and Recipient will enter on discussions regarding future collaboration toward European Funded Project in the field of [….] or [will submit a proposal for a collaborative project in response to the call (identify the call) under (identify the EU funded Programme)] or [will enter into or evaluate alternatives for partnership or collaboration with [name of the Recipient] for the purpose of [identify the undertaking intended to perform or achieve].
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Purpose of the Disclosure of Confidential Information The Discloser and Recipient will engage in discussions. Specific purposes may be agreed upon separately.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specifics of the collaboration, the clause creates an in-text contradiction by generating ambiguity in the 'permitted purposes' mentioned in Section 3.1. Without defined purposes, the restriction on using Confidential Information 'solely in connection with purposes contemplated in this Agreement' becomes unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 In the context of discussions, preparations or negotiations, the Discloser may disclose Confidential Information to the Recipient. T h e R e c i p i e n t a g r e e s t o u s e t h e Confidential Information solely in connection with purposes contemplated in this Agreement and not to use it for any other purpose or without the prior written consent of the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified purposes for disclosing confidential information. The changed text removes these specific purposes, stating they may be agreed upon separately. This creates a contradiction with Section 3.1, which requires the recipient to use Confidential Information 'solely in connection with purposes contemplated in this Agreement.' Since the purposes are no longer clearly defined within the agreement itself (due to the change), it's unclear what constitutes a 'permitted purpose', thereby creating ambiguity and making the restriction unenforceable, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Discloser may request it to the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Recipient shall return or destroy Confidential Information as reasonably requested by the Discloser. The method of return or destruction will be subject to mutual agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific triggers for returning/destroying confidential information (completion/termination), this amendment creates a contradiction because Section 4.1.1 specifies a duration for the agreement, and the Recipient's duty to hold Confidential Information remains in effect 'indefinitely.' This creates an inconsistency, because the Recipient has an 'indefinite' duty but no clear guidance (now omitted) on *when* to return or destroy the information. This affects enforcement, because compliance is now subjective and dependent on the Discloser's request.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for a term of [number of months or years] term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified clear triggers for returning/destroying confidential information, including the completion or termination of the agreement. The changed text replaces these specific triggers with a requirement to return or destroy confidential information 'as reasonably requested by the Discloser,' and requires mutual agreement for the method. This omission creates a contradiction with Section 4.1.1, which states that the Recipient’s duty to hold Confidential Information remains in effect 'indefinitely.' The lack of specific triggers in the changed text means the Recipient's indefinite duty may not be fulfillable, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.10 The Recipient agrees that the Discloser will suffer irreparable damage if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such a breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.10 The Recipient acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information may cause damage. The Discloser reserves the right to pursue remedies.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific entitlement to 'injunctive relief' and 'exemplary damages,' this creates an in-text contradiction. While Section 4.2 specifies the governing law and jurisdiction, the omission weakens the Discloser's explicitly defined remedies. This causes confusion, because while remedies are generally available under the governing law, the originally stated, more specific remedies are now absent, making their availability less certain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Applicable Law and Jurisdiction This Agreement shall be construed and interpreted by the laws of [choose the applicable law]. The court of [choose the jurisdiction to settle disputes] shall have jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly entitled the Discloser to 'injunctive relief' and 'exemplary damages'. The changed text weakens this by stating only that 'The Discloser reserves the right to pursue remedies'. This creates a contradiction because while Section 4.2 states the applicable law and jurisdiction, the removal of the explicitly stated remedies makes their availability uncertain under the contract, potentially conflicting with what was initially agreed upon and understood.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.2 If the Discloser and Recipient succeed in the call for proposal referred to in clause 2 and sign the corresponding Grant Agreement (GA) and Consortium Agreement (CA), or entered into partnership under any other kind of collaborative agreement (COA) or association agreement (AA), the non-disclosure provisions of the CA, COA and AA shall [supplement or supersede] this Agreement. In the event that non-disclosure provisions are not provided for the said private agreements in equal terms as stated herein, this Agreement shall remain in force until the end of the collaboration undertaken or after [months or years] of its termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.2 If the Discloser and Recipient enter into a future agreement, its non-disclosure provisions shall take precedence. This Agreement will then terminate.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed conditions regarding Grant Agreements, Consortium Agreements, and alternative collaborative agreements, the changed text creates an in-text contradiction. The original Section 4.1.1 states that the Recipient's duty to hold Confidential Information remains in effect 'indefinitely,' save otherwise agreed. However, the modified Section 4.1.2 now simply states that 'This Agreement will then terminate,' omitting the extended obligation period if the private agreements' terms aren't equal. This creates uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1.1 This Agreement shall remain in effect for a term of [number of months or years] term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that if future agreements lack non-disclosure provisions equal to those in the agreement, this agreement remains in force. The changed text eliminates this provision, stating the agreement terminates, which contradicts the original agreement's indefinite confidentiality duty outlined in Section 4.1.1, as the Recipient's duty to hold Confidential Information that was disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely. Therefore, the change directly conflicts with the contradicted section, creating an in-text contradiction.
